topics: Classification of elements in the Periodic Table
Formation of compounds based on atomic structure (Bohr's model) and properties (valency)
Nature and properties of chemical substances (distillation, crystallisation, chromatography, centrifugation, mixtures, solutions, colloids, suspensions)
Chemical interactions and changes using symbols and chemical equations (acid-base, metal-non-metal, reversible-irreversible)
key_concepts: CG 1: Explores the world of matter, its interactions, and properties at the atomic level
C 1.2: Investigates nature and properties of chemical substances

units.unit_name: Unit 2: Physical World and Scientific Principles
topics: Newton's laws and effect of forces (displacement, direction, velocity, acceleration, uniform circular motion)
Graphical and mathematical representations of motion in one dimension
Relationship between mass and weight using universal law of gravitation
Laws of motion connection
Image characteristics with lenses (focus, centre of curvature) and ray diagrams
Combination of lenses (telescope, microscope)
Circuit characteristics (current, voltage, resistance) and Ohm's law
Everyday usage of electricity (electricity bill, short circuit, safety measures)
Work in scientific terms and relationship between potential and kinetic energy (conservation of energy)
Mechanical advantage through simple machines (levers and pulleys)
Origin and properties of sound (wavelength, frequency, amplitude)
Propagation of sound through different instruments
Interconnected systems supporting life on Earth (hydrosphere, biosphere, atmosphere, geosphere, cryosphere)
key_concepts: CG 2: Explores the physical world around them, and understands scientific principles and laws based on observations and analysis
C 2.5: Defines work and energy conservation

units.unit_name: Unit 3: Living World at Cellular Level
topics: Role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall)
Semi-permeability of cell membrane
key_concepts: CG 3: Explores the structure and function of the living world at the cellular level
C 3.1: Explains role of cellular components and cell as structural basis of life

units.unit_name: Life Processes and Heredity (Implicit Unit based on CG 3)
topics: C 3.2 – Analyses similarities and differences in the life processes involved in nutrition, transport, exchange of materials, and reproduction
C 3.3 – Describes the mechanisms of heredity (in terms of DNA, genes, chromosomes) and variation
subtopics: Photosynthesis in plants; absorption of nutrients in fungi; digestion in animals
Transport of water in plants; circulation in animals
Changes in the sequence of DNA

units.unit_name: Organisms and Environment (Implicit Unit based on CG 4)
topics: C 4.1 – Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play to classify them into five kingdoms
C 4.2 – Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms)
C 4.3 – Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level
C 4.4 – Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws and its consequences at a population level
C 4.5 – Analyses evidences of biological evolution demonstrating the consequences of the process of natural selection
subtopics: Biological organisation (organisms to ecosystems and biomes)

units.unit_name: Interdisciplinary Connections (Implicit Unit based on CG 5)
topics: C 5.1 – Explores how literature and arts have influenced Science
C 5.2 – Examines a case study related to the use of Science in human life from the perspective of Social Sciences and Ethics
C 5.3 – Applies scientific principles to explain phenomena in other subjects
subtopics: Literature and arts influence on Science
Case studies: Marie Curie, Jenner, treatment of patients with mental illnesses, atomic bomb, green revolution, GMOs, conservation of biodiversity
Sound pitch, octave, and amplitude in music
Use of muscles in dance form and sports

units.unit_name: Indian Contributions to Science (Implicit Unit based on CG 6)
topics: C 6.1 – Knows and explains the significant contributions of India to all matters studied within the curriculum in an integrated manner

units.unit_name: Current Discoveries and Frontiers (Implicit Unit based on CG 7)
topics: C 7.1 – States concepts that represent the most current understanding of the matter being studied
C 7.2 – States questions related to matters in the curriculum for which current scientific understanding is well-recognised
subtopics: Conceptual understanding appropriate to developmental stage

units.unit_name: Nature of Science (Implicit Unit based on CG 8)
topics: C 8.1 – Develops accurate and appropriate models to represent real-life events and phenomena using scientific principles
Chemical Reactions and Equations
Periodic Classification of Elements
Acids, Bases and Salts
Metals and Non-metals
Carbon and its Compounds
subtopics: Chemical reactions, Chemical equation, Balanced chemical equation, types of chemical reactions: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation, endothermic exothermic reactions, oxidation and reduction
Döbereiner's Triads, Newlands' Law of Octaves, Mendeléev's Periodic Table, Modern Periodic Table and the Modern, Metallic and Non-metallic Properties

Acids and Bases – definitions in terms of furnishing of H+ and OH- ions, identification using indicators, chemical properties, examples and uses, neutralization, concept of pH scale (Definition relating to logarithm not required), importance of pH in everyday life; preparation and uses of Sodium Hydroxide, Bleaching Powder, Baking soda, Washing soda and Plaster of Paris
Properties of metals and non-metals; Reactivity series; Formation and properties of ionic compounds; Basic metallurgical processes; Corrosion and its prevention

Covalent bonds – formation and properties of covalent compounds, Versatile nature of carbon, Hydrocarbons – saturated and unsaturated Homologous series. Nomenclature of alkanes, alkenes, alkyne and carbon compounds containing functional groups (halogens, alcohol, ketones, aldehydes). Chemical properties of carbon compounds (combustion, oxidation, addition and substitution reaction). Ethanol and Ethanoic acid (only properties and uses), soaps and detergents

topics: Life processes
Control and co-ordination in animals and plants
Reproduction
Heredity
subtopics: 'Living Being'. Basic concept of nutrition, respiration, transport and excretion in plants and animals
Tropic movements in plants; Introduction of plant hormones; Control and co-ordination in animals: Nervous system; Voluntary, involuntary and reflex action; Chemical co-ordination: animal hormones
Reproduction in animals and plants (asexual and sexual) reproductive health - need and methods of family planning. Safe sex vs HIV/AIDS. Child bearing and women's health
Heredity; Mendel's contribution- Laws for inheritance of traits: Sex determination; brief introduction
key_concepts: Control and co-ordination in animals and plants

topics: Reflection of light by curved surfaces
Refraction
Refraction of light by spherical lens
subtopics: Images formed by spherical mirrors, centre of curvature, principal axis, principal focus, focal length, mirror formula (Derivation not required), magnification
Image formed by spherical lenses; Lens formula (Derivation not required); Magnification. Power of a lens
key_concepts: Reflection of light by curved surfaces
Refraction of light by spherical lens

topics: Functioning of a lens in human eye, defects of vision and their corrections, applications of spherical mirrors and lenses
Refraction of light through a prism, dispersion of light, scattering of light, applications in daily life (excluding colour of the sun at sunrise and sunset)
Electric current, potential difference and electric current. Ohm's law; Resistance, Resistivity, Factors on which the resistance of a conductor depends
Series combination of resistors, parallel combination of resistors and its applications in daily life
Heating effect of electric current and its applications in daily life
Electric power, Interrelation between P, V, I and R

Magnetic effects of current: Magnetic field, field lines, field due to a current carrying conductor, field due to current carrying coil or solenoid; Force on current carrying conductor, Fleming's Left Hand Rule, Direct current
Alternating current: frequency of AC. Advantage of AC over DC. Domestic electric circuits
Motor, Electromagnetic Induction, Electric Generator
key_concepts: Series and Parallel combination of resistors

topics: Our environment: Eco-system, Environmental problems, Ozone depletion, waste production and their solutions
Biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances

Finding the pH of the following samples by using pH paper/universal indicator: Dilute Hydrochloric Acid, Dilute NaOH solution, Dilute Ethanoic Acid solution, Lemon juice, Water, Dilute Hydrogen Carbonate solution
Studying the properties of acids and bases (HCl & NaOH) on the basis of their reaction with: Litmus solution (Blue/Red), Zinc metal, Solid sodium carbonate
Performing and observing the following reactions and classifying them into: Combination reaction, Decomposition reaction, Displacement reaction, Double displacement reaction
Action of water on quicklime
Action of heat on ferrous sulphate crystals
Iron nails kept in copper sulphate solution
Reaction between sodium sulphate and barium chloride solutions
Observing the action of Zn, Fe, Cu and Al metals on the following salt solutions: ZnSO4 (aq), FeSO4 (aq), CuSO4 (aq), Al2(SO4)3 (aq)
Arranging Zn, Fe, Cu and Al (metals) in the decreasing order of reactivity based on the above result
Studying the dependence of potential difference (V) across a resistor on the current (I) passing through it and determine its resistance. Also plotting a graph between V and I
Study of the following properties of acetic acid (ethanoic acid): Odour, solubility in water, effect on litmus, reaction with Sodium Hydrogen Carbonate
Preparing a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata
Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration
Studying binary fission in Amoeba, and budding in yeast and Hydra with the help of prepared slides
Determination of the focal length of Concave mirror and Convex lens by obtaining the image of a distant object

Tracing the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab for different angles of incidence. Measure the angle of incidence, angle of refraction, angle of emergence and interpret the result
Tracing the path of the rays of light through a glass prism
Determination of the equivalent resistance of two resistors when connected in series and parallel
Identification of the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed (pea, gram or red kidney bean)
Study of the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water
